headline: Bidayuh Villages Lack Road Access After 60 Years
context: Survey found 45 Bidayuh villages in Sarawak Bau and Serian divisions still have no paved road access in 2026.

cards.big: 45 Bidayuh villages in Sarawak still have no paved road access
cards.sub: Communities of 200-500 people rely on logging tracks impassable during rain

cards.big: State rural road budget of RM320 million covers only 180km new roads per year
cards.sub: At current pace the 45 villages would wait until 2038 for basic road connections

cards.big: Ambulance response to these villages averages 4 hours versus 18 minutes urban
cards.sub: 3 maternal deaths in Tebedu in 2025 attributed to delayed medical transport

cards.big: GPS allocated RM85 million for Bidayuh area roads in the 2026 state budget
cards.sub: Community leaders say actual construction begins years after budget announcements

cards.big: Road access gaps reveal whether rural Borneo communities are visible to planners
60 years without road access for Bidayuh villages. Basic connectivity is not a development luxury.
